The Start with a Spark campaign invites K-12 school professionals to share what’s inspiring them this year and gives them a chance to win funding for their classroom wishlists.

Analysis-Autopilot verdict deals Tesla a 'black eye', threatens Musk's robotaxi ambitions SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -A court verdict against Tesla last week, stemming from a fatal 2019 crash of an Autopilot-equipped Model S, could hurt its plans to expand its nascent robotaxi network and intensify concerns over the safety of its autonomous vehicle technology.  A Florida jury ordered Musk's electric vehicle company on Friday to pay about $243 million to victims of the crash, finding its Autopilot driver-assistance software defective.  Tesla said the driver was solely at fault and vowed to appeal.
